content.problemStatement:
Gru has been ordering the minions to do a lot of work lately. Minions don't want to trust Gru anymore. So, they propose a problem to Gru. They will only help Gru if he can answer the problem correctly. The minions  generate an endless string sss by following these steps:


Let sss be an empty string.
i=1i = 1i=1.
Append iii a s to the back of sss.
Append iii b s to the back of sss.
Multiply iii by 222 and go back to the third step.
So sss will be abaabbaaaabbbb.... Now let's define f(x)f(x)f(x) as number of distinct palindrome substring segments in s[1…x]s[1 \ldots x]s[1…x]. For example, f(3)=4f(3) = 4f(3)=4 because a (which is s[1…1]s[1 \ldots 1]s[1…1]), b (which is s[2…2]s[2 \ldots 2]s[2…2]), a (which is s[3…3]s[3 \ldots 3]s[3…3]) and aba (which is s[1…3]s[1 \ldots 3]s[1…3]) are possible palindrome substrings of s[1…3]s[1 \ldots 3]s[1…3].

The minions will give an integer NNN. Help Gru calculate (∑i=1Nf(i)) mod (109+7)\sum_{i=1}^{N} f(i)) \bmod{({10}^9 + 7)}∑i=1N​f(i))mod(109+7).

Note: We are using 1-indexing.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains a single line of input, which has one integer, NNN.
###Output:

For each testcase, output in a single line, the answer modulo (109+7)({10}^9 + 7)(109+7).

###Constraints


1≤T≤1031 \leq T \leq 10^31≤T≤103
1≤N≤10181 \leq N \leq 10^{18}1≤N≤1018
###Sample Input:
1
3

###Sample Output:
7

###EXPLANATION:
f(1)=1f(1) = 1f(1)=1, f(2)=2f(2) = 2f(2)=2, f(3)=4f(3) = 4f(3)=4. And so, the answer for N=3N = 3N=3 is 1+2+4=71+2+4 = 71+2+4=7.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T. Each test case contains a single line of input, which has one integer, N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output in a single line, the answer modulo (10^9 + 7).

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N = 1, the answer is 1. For N = 2, the answer is 1 + 2 = 3. For N = 3, the answer is 1 + 2 + 4 = 7.

solution.approach: We use dynamic programming to calculate the cumulative palindrome count. We maintain a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] represents the number of distinct palindrome substrings of length j ending at index i. We iterate over the string and update the dp array accordingly. Finally, we calculate the answer by summing up the values in the dp array.
